channel.title: AI Campaign News (AICN)
channel.description: A tracker of AI use in political campaigns, elections, and advocacy.

item.title: Tech: OpenAI warms up to Washington
item.description: Punchbowl News reports that OpenAI has backed Sen. Marsha Blackburn's Kids Online Safety Act and the NO FAKES Act, signaling a warmer relationship between the company and Washington lawmakers heading into the 2026 cycle.

item.title: 'Who should I vote for?' Voters turn to AI before casting their ballots
item.description: The Philadelphia Inquirer reports that voters are using AI chatbots to research candidates and guide their voting decisions ahead of the 2026 midterms, even though the tools are designed to avoid expressing political opinions.

item.title: 30 States Now Have Laws to Regulate Election Deepfakes
item.description: Public Citizen reports that 30 states have now enacted laws regulating AI-generated deepfakes in election communications, with Maryland Governor Wes Moore's signature on a new law marking the most recent addition to that count.

item.title: Tech: What unions want on AI policy
item.description: Punchbowl News reports that labor union leaders, framing AI-driven automation as an existential threat, are planning aggressive engagement in Washington AI policy fights and in the 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: Gavin Newsom treads the AI line
item.description: Semafor reports that California Governor Gavin Newsom is walking a careful line between pushing to regulate the power of major tech companies while simultaneously using AI products in his own political operations.

item.title: Get Ready To See a Lot More AI in Campaign Ads
item.description: The Dispatch surveys the growing use of AI in campaign advertising, finding that while some campaign staffers are embracing AI to amplify messaging, others are raising ethical questions about the strategy, with deepfakes specifically mentioned as a concern.

item.title: Donald Trump's Improvised AI Regime
item.description: The Dispatch characterizes the Trump administration's approach to AI governance as improvised and ad hoc rather than a coherent regulatory strategy, raising questions about how AI policy will be set heading into the 2026 elections.

item.title: Anthropic Added a New Security Measure to Get Back Into the Trump Administration’s Good Graces
item.description: Following a prior freeze, the Trump administration removed export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models after Anthropic added a new security measure, marking a partial repair of the strained relationship between the company and the White House.

item.title: ‘The Battle Line Has Been Drawn’ Around Virginia’s Data Centers
item.description: Virginia state lawmakers have moved to regulate the data center industry in Northern Virginia amid souring public sentiment, creating what NOTUS describes as a first-of-its-kind tax on the sector as the political backlash against AI infrastructure reaches the statehouse level.

item.title: A New AI Tool Targets the Least-Loved Task in Public Affairs: the Weekly Client Report
item.description: Campaigns & Elections features a new AI tool designed to automate the weekly client report process for public affairs and lobbying professionals, addressing a time-intensive task as retainer scopes have expanded.

item.title: The Trump White House Is Over Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei
item.description: Wired reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has been replaced by cofounder Tom Brown as the company's representative in high-stakes White House meetings, with one official describing Amodei as a "weirdo." The shift signals a cooling relationship between Anthropic's leadership and the Trump administration.

item.title: The Anti-Data-Center Movement Is Reshaping Michigan Politics
item.description: Sunrise Movement cofounder Will Lawrence is running for a Michigan swing-district congressional seat with a call for a data center moratorium as a central campaign issue, reflecting grassroots opposition to AI infrastructure development entering electoral politics.

item.title: Senate Committee Advances Bill to Protect Name, Image, Likeness and Voice Against Unauthorized AI Use
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee advanced the NO FAKES Act, legislation that would create a federal right protecting individuals' name, image, likeness, and voice from unauthorized AI-generated reproductions. The Holland & Knight analysis summarizes the bill's key provisions as it moves toward a full Senate vote.

item.title: NO FAKES Act clears Senate panel; judge blocks SNAP soda and candy limits; quantum math sharpens cancer AI — Morning Medical Update
item.description: A morning news roundup notes that the NO FAKES Act cleared its Senate panel, confirming the committee advancement reported in more detail elsewhere.

item.title: NGP VAN Announces New AI-Assisted Fundraising Tools: Donor Target Scores and Donor Target Reports
item.description: NGP VAN announced two new AI-assisted fundraising features — Donor Target Scores and Donor Target Reports — designed to help campaigns prioritize outreach to likely donors using predictive modeling.

item.title: Nexxen partners with L2 Data to launch voter targeting tool
item.description: Ad-tech company Nexxen has partnered with political data firm L2 to launch a voter targeting tool, enabling campaigns and political advertisers to reach audiences matched against L2's voter file data.

item.title: AI and tech are trying to influence the midterm elections
item.description: NPR reports that major AI companies, including Anthropic and OpenAI, are actively spending and lobbying to influence how their tools are used in the 2026 midterm elections, with congressional outreach and campaign-facing deployments under scrutiny.

item.title: AI Companies Are Trying to Seize Control of Elections
item.description: Futurism argues that AI companies are positioning themselves to exert significant influence over the 2026 midterms through campaign tools, data products, and political relationships, framing this as a structural threat to electoral integrity.

item.title: Senate Judiciary advances NO FAKES Act on unanimous vote
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee passed the NO FAKES Act by unanimous vote, advancing federal legislation that would create a right of publicity against unauthorized AI-generated replicas of a person's name, image, likeness, or voice.

item.title: No Fakes Act Clears Senate Judiciary Committee
item.description: Deadline covers the Senate Judiciary Committee's approval of the NO FAKES Act, noting its implications for AI-generated content across entertainment and political contexts.

item.title: Nexxen and L2 Data Launch VoterMatch, Bringing Greater Precision, Transparency and Performance to Political Advertising
item.description: Nexxen and L2 Data announced VoterMatch, a joint product combining L2's voter file data with Nexxen's programmatic advertising platform to enable more precise political ad targeting for campaigns and outside groups.

item.title: AI and tech are trying to influence the midterm elections
item.description: NPR reports that major AI companies including Anthropic and OpenAI are increasing lobbying efforts and political spending ahead of the 2026 midterm elections, aiming to influence policy and the broader electoral environment. The piece examines how the tech sector is positioning itself to shape how AI is regulated and used in campaigns.

item.title: AI Companies Are Trying to Seize Control of Elections
item.description: Futurism covers the broader pattern of AI companies attempting to exert influence over the 2026 midterm election cycle through lobbying, political donations, and direct engagement with candidates and policymakers. The piece frames this as an industry-wide push to shape the regulatory and political landscape.

item.title: This Democratic Primary Will Test if Voters Care About AI
item.description: A New York Democratic congressional primary featured a candidate who built his campaign around AI policy expertise, making the race a real-world test of whether voters reward candidates who center artificial intelligence as a political issue. The outcome offered early data on AI's salience as a campaign issue.

item.title: Senate Judiciary advances NO FAKES Act on unanimous vote
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee advanced the NO FAKES Act on a unanimous vote, moving federal legislation forward that would establish rights against unauthorized use of a person's name, image, likeness, or voice by AI systems. The bill now heads to the full Senate.

item.title: The NO FAKES Act Could Silence Satire, Commentary, And News
item.description: The Electronic Frontier Foundation argues that the NO FAKES Act, while targeting harmful AI-generated likenesses, is drafted broadly enough to suppress legitimate satire, political commentary, and journalism about public figures. The EFF calls for narrower carve-outs to protect First Amendment activity.

item.title: Senate Committee Advances Bill to Protect Name, Image, Likeness and Voice Against Unauthorized AI Use
item.description: Law firm Holland & Knight provides a legal analysis of the Senate Judiciary Committee's advancement of the NO FAKES Act, outlining what the bill would require of platforms and AI developers regarding unauthorized synthetic replicas of real individuals.

item.title: NGP VAN Rolls Out New AI Compliance Review Tool
item.description: NGP VAN, a leading Democratic campaign technology provider, launched a new AI-powered compliance review tool designed to help campaigns check materials against legal and regulatory requirements. The tool is positioned as an efficiency aid for campaign compliance operations.

item.title: Civis Analytics has achieved the AWS Generative AI Competency
item.description: Civis Analytics announced it has achieved the AWS Generative AI Competency designation, indicating the political data and analytics firm has met Amazon's standards for deploying generative AI solutions on its cloud platform.

item.title: Nexxen & L2 Data launch VoterMatch for political ads
item.description: Ad-tech firm Nexxen and political data provider L2 Data launched VoterMatch, a product that enables more precise targeting of political advertisements by matching voter file data to digital advertising audiences. The tool is aimed at increasing the effectiveness of political ad buys.
